question_text: A 4 kg object is placed on Earth (g = 10 N/kg) and then on Jupiter (g = 25 N/kg). Calculate the object's weight on both planets and determine how many times heavier the object is on Jupiter compared to Earth.
correct_answer: Weight on Earth = 4 × 10 = 40 N. Weight on Jupiter = 4 × 25 = 100 N. The object is 100 ÷ 40 = 2.5 times heavier on Jupiter than on Earth.
explanation: Using W = mg for both planets shows how gravitational field strength directly affects weight. Jupiter's stronger gravity (g = 25 N/kg) means objects weigh 2.5 times more there than on Earth, even though their mass remains unchanged.
